title: Business stakeholders
summary: 9609 AS — internal/external stakeholders, aims, conflicts, and stakeholder mapping.
learningObjectives: Define stakeholder and distinguish from shareholder.
Identify internal and external stakeholder groups.

simpleExplanation.title: Everyone with a stake
simpleExplanation.summary: Stakeholders are groups affected by or able to affect a business. Internal stakeholders include owners and employees; external include customers, suppliers, government, and community. Their aims often conflict.
steps: Conflicts: profit vs wages vs low prices.
Stakeholder map: power vs interest grid.

flashcards.back: Any group with interest in or affected by business activities.
Shareholder owns shares; stakeholder is broader — includes employees, customers, etc.
Owners, managers, employees — inside the organisation.
Customers, suppliers, creditors, government, local community.
Manage closely (high power + interest); keep satisfied; monitor; minimal effort.
Wants reliable orders and prompt payment.
Tax, jobs, legal compliance, economic stability.

sections.content: **Business stakeholders** (1.5.1) opens **Topic 1.5**. **Stakeholder** ≠ **shareholder** — examiners check you know the difference.

sections.question: Factory plans automation cutting 200 jobs but raising profit by $3m. Identify three stakeholder groups and their likely views.
sections.solution:
**Shareholders:** **Support** — **$3m profit** rise, higher **dividends** and share price.

**Employees (and unions):** **Oppose** — **200 redundancies**, community unemployment; may **strike** (2.1.7).

**Local community:** **Mixed** — lost spending power vs **tax base** if firm stays profitable.

**Customers:** **Neutral/positive** if **quality/cost** improves; **negative** if service suffers during transition.

**Government:** **Concern** over **unemployment** — may offer **retraining grants**; wants **tax revenue** maintained.
